not_supported under the frozen rule: primary Spearman rho between decision-time standardized DD center separation and next-selection-month switch count was +0.035/+0.320/+0.155 in US/DE/JP with one-sided permutation p 0.68/1.00/0.98 (10000 draws, seed 20260722), so rho >= 0 in at least two markets. Descriptive mechanism: collapsed one-state months (0/43/63 in US/DE/JP) have zero switches by construction and pull the JP association positive (fitted-only JP rho = -0.142, DE stays +0.303, US +0.035); DE separation therefore behaves as a regime-stress coincident proxy, not a stability signal. Separation offers no support for a turnover gate; per the frozen contract no gate formula is proposed. Evidence: artifacts/separation-turnover-001/separation-turnover-8674ff4d9470-20260722T083551Z (table CSV, summary JSON, chart) computed only from the sealed dd_scaled_3x refits/choices/trades of run dd-loss-scale-e1e84ddbbdda-65ccb507abba-20260722T045053128156Z; join, partition, cutoff, and switch-total consistency checks all passed in-run
